BUSINESS GLOSSARY - Enterprise System
Last Updated: 2024-03-15
Status: Partial (pending review)

==========================================
CUSTOMER MANAGEMENT
==========================================

Customer
An individual or organization that... [definition incomplete]

Client
A person or company that purchases goods or services. (Note: may be used interchangeably with Customer in some contexts)

Account Holder
The person designated as the primary owner of an account. Similar to Customer bu... [TRUNCATED]

triplets.subject: Client
triplets.object: A person or company that purchases goods or services

triplets.subject: Account Holder
triplets.object: The person designated as the primary owner of an account

triplets.subject: End User
triplets.object: The actual user of a product or service

triplets.subject: Order
triplets.object: A request to purchase products or services

triplets.subject: Transaction
triplets.object: Similar to Order but more general

triplets.subject: Sales Order
triplets.object: An order with confirmed pricing and inventory allocation

triplets.subject: Quote
triplets.object: A preliminary order with estimated pricing

triplets.subject: SKU
triplets.object: Stock Keeping Unit - a unique identifier for a product variant

triplets.subject: Payment
triplets.object: The transfer of money to settle an order

triplets.subject: Shipment
triplets.object: The process of delivering goods to a customer

triplets.subject: Delivery
triplets.object: The completion of a Shipment when goods reach the customer

triplets.subject: Fulfillment
triplets.object: The process of preparing and delivering orders

entities_post_resolution.name: Customers.Customer Id
entities_post_resolution.definition: The unique identifier for a customer, stored as the primary key in the CUSTOMERS table and used as a foreign key by related tables (e.g., ORDERS).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Foreign Keys:
- customer_id → CUSTOMERS(customer_id or CustomerID?) | | Column Name | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|-------------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| customer_id | INTEGER | PK | [missing documentation] |
| CustomerID | INTEGER | ? | Possible duplicate of customer_id | | Primary Key: customer_id (or is it CustomerID? check with DBA) | | Column Name | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|-------------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| order_id | INTEGER | PK | Unique order identifier |
| OrderID | INTEGER | ? | [possible duplicate] |
| customer_id | INTEGER | FK | References customer (which table?) |
| CustomerID | INTEGER | FK | [FK reference unclear] | | Foreign Keys:
- customer_id → CUSTOMERS(customer_id or CustomerID?)
- [missing other FK definitions] | | customer_id | INTEGER | FK | References CUSTOMERS |

entities_post_resolution.name: Financial Transaction
entities_post_resolution.definition: A financial transaction is a monetary exchange between parties.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Financial Transaction
Any monetary exchange. See Payment, Transaction. | Transaction
Similar to Order but more general. May refer to any exchange of value.

entities_post_resolution.name: Order Date
entities_post_resolution.definition: The timestamp indicating when an order was created, representing the order creation date/time in the business data model.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
| Column Name | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|-------------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| order_date | DATE | ? | Date order was created |
| OrderDate | DATETIME | ? | [different precision?] |

entities_post_resolution.name: Order Id
entities_post_resolution.definition: The primary key column that uniquely identifies an order in the orders table.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Primary Key: order_id (or OrderID - verify) | | Column Name | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|-------------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| order_id | INTEGER | PK | Unique order identifier |
| OrderID | INTEGER | ? | [possible duplicate] | | | order_id | INTEGER | FK | References ORDERS |

entities_post_resolution.name: Product Id
entities_post_resolution.definition: The product identifier used as a foreign key to reference a product in the PRODUCTS (or INVENTORY) catalog, as observed in related tables such as order items.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
| product_id | INTEGER | FK | References PRODUCTS (or INVENTORY?) | | | order_item_id | INTEGER | PK | [missing documentation] |
| order_id | INTEGER | FK | References ORDERS |
| product_id | INTEGER | FK | References PRODUCTS (or INVENTORY?) | | | product_id | INTEGER | FK | References PRODUCTS (or INVENTORY?) |
| ProductID | INTEGER | FK | [inconsistent naming] | | | ProductID | INTEGER | FK | [inconsistent naming] |

entities_post_resolution.name: Order With Confirmed
entities_post_resolution.definition: A sales order in which pricing has been confirmed and inventory has been allocated.
synonyms: A preliminary order with estimated pricing
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Sales Order
An order with confirmed pricing and inventory allocation. Distinct from Quote. | Quote
A preliminary order with estimated pricing, not yet confirmed.

entities_post_resolution.name: Decimal(10,2)
entities_post_resolution.definition: A decimal numeric data type with precision 12 and scale 2 used to store monetary values (e.g., line totals) in business records.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | discount_amount | DECIMAL(10,2) | ? | Calculated discount | | | line_total | DECIMAL(12,2) | ? | Line item total | | | LineTotal | DECIMAL(12,2) | ? | [duplicate?] | | | discount_percent | DECIMAL(5,2) | ? | Discount percentage |

entities_post_resolution.name: Process Of Preparing
entities_post_resolution.definition: The end-to-end business process of preparing and delivering customer orders to fulfill them, including the shipment and delivery steps.
synonyms: The process of delivering goods to a customer
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Fulfillment
The process of preparing and delivering orders. Encompasses Shipment and Delivery. | Delivery
The completion of a Shipment when goods reach the customer. | Shipment
The process of delivering goods to a customer.

entities_post_resolution.name: Physical Products Held
entities_post_resolution.definition: Inventory items consisting of physical goods held in stock that are available for sale.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Inventory Item
Physical products held in stock. May be referenced as Product or Item. | Merchandise
Goods available for sale. Similar to Product.

entities_post_resolution.name: Revenue From Selling Products
entities_post_resolution.definition: The revenue generated from selling products (i.e., income from sales).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Sales
Revenue from selling products. See Revenue. | Revenue
Income from sales. The same as... [definition circular, see Sales]

tables_enriched.name: Customer
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about customers, including identifiers, contact details, account status, type, and regional information.

tables_enriched.name: Orders
tables_enriched.description: Stores order transactions including identifiers, customer association, dates, status, payment details, monetary amounts (order, total, shipping, tax), and audit notes. Used to track and summarize cust... [TRUNCATED]

tables_enriched.name: Order Items
tables_enriched.description: Stores items that belong to orders, including product references, quantities, pricing, discounts, and the calculated line totals for each order item.

tables_enriched.name: Products
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about products, including identifiers, names, SKUs, categories, pricing, stock levels, and descriptions, to support product catalog, pricing, inventory, and activation status.

tables_enriched.name: Payments
tables_enriched.description: Stores records of payments processed, including identifiers, dates, amounts, methods, statuses, related orders and customers, and transaction responses.
